steps.title: Map the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 says the shifting has been attributed to passive displacement by water currents. Sentence 2 reports that the barnacles moved toward the turtles' heads, against the prevailing flow, consistent with self-initiated locomotion. The finding contradicts the passive-displacement explanation.

steps.title: Pick a transition that signals contradiction of the explanation
steps.content: The blank needs a transition meaning the new finding weakens the explanation just given. "Undermining this explanation" fits exactly.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• "Contrary to this phenomenon,": misreads what is being opposed; the research opposes the explanation for the phenomenon, not the phenomenon (the shifting) itself, which still occurs. ✗
  • "Undermining this explanation,": correctly signals that the new finding weakens the passive-current explanation. ✓
  • "Drawing a similar conclusion,": overreach in the wrong direction; the new finding reaches an opposite conclusion (active movement), not a similar one. ✗
  • "Confirming this hypothesis,": reverses the relationship; the finding refutes, not confirms, the passive-displacement hypothesis. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
